notes: Measured Phase 2 synthetic B512 prompt608 cells for measurement analysis only. The FastTTS manual planner no longer consumes this artifact; automatic hybrid policy selection is deferred until the general hybrid/weight-offload policy is pinned down.

cells.label: llama-eager-0.74-split736-lower-boundary-negative
cells.notes: Lower-memory boundary probe below the 0.75 loss. Hybrid remains negative, so no lower-memory gain cliff appeared at 0.74.

cells.label: llama-eager-0.72-split736-lower-boundary-negative
cells.notes: Lower-memory boundary probe below 0.74. Hybrid remains negative even with a small effective-capacity increase, reinforcing that lower memory is not sufficient.

cells.label: llama-eager-0.72-split640-frontier-repeat-win
cells.notes: Capacity-window split-frontier sweep. At mem0.72, fixed split736 loses, but split640 crosses a useful scheduler wave and repeats as a 1.03x hybrid win over GPU-only.

cells.label: llama-eager-0.80-total1024-split1008-one-block-negative
cells.notes: One-block active-suffix control. Exact no-suffix hybrid split1024 is rejected because COTS hybrid KV requires at least one CPU suffix block.

cells.label: llama-eager-0.80-total1024-split960-suffix-admission-pause-negative
cells.notes: Env-gated suffix-active admission pause was tested and removed. It reduced some mixed-prefix admission but starved useful waiting admission, leaving queued requests while GPU KV was underused in several windows; throughput regressed versus default hybrid.

cells.label: llama-eager-0.78-split736-boundary-negative
cells.notes: Boundary probe between known Llama 0.75 loss and 0.80 win; still negative, so relative capacity gain alone is not enough.

cells.label: llama-eager-0.79-split736-boundary-negative
cells.notes: Boundary probe near the known Llama 0.80 win; still below GPU-only, indicating the positive cell depends on the discrete effective-capacity point around 30x.

cells.label: llama-eager-split736-0.79-vs-0.80-scheduler-shape
cells.notes: Stats-enabled diagnostic. The 0.80 point admits a larger initial wave (281 vs 257 running) and both GPU-only and hybrid improve; hybrid crosses GPU-only only at this higher realized-capacity point.

cells.label: qwen-graph-0.67-split672-vs-split704-scheduler-shape
cells.graph_note: GPU-only used FULL_AND_PIECEWISE graph mode; COTS hybrid defaulted to PIECEWISE graph mode.
cells.notes: Stats-enabled diagnostic. Qwen graph split672 wins because moving the split earlier raises effective capacity enough to create a larger useful running wave; split704 has too little capacity gain and pays suffix/UVA overhead.

cells.label: llama-indexed-queryskip-overhead-ablation
cells.notes: Indexed merge alone did not move the 0.75 near-miss. Skipping an unnecessary GPU query index_select when staged CPU query/QKV is valid reduced wrapper overhead by about 1%, but 0.75 still loses to a same-build GPU-only run. The 0.72/split640 capacity-window win remains positive.

cells.label: llama-coalesced-overlap-negative-ablation
cells.notes: Submitting CPU suffix before the coalesced GPU prefix regressed the 0.75/split736 near-miss by about 0.8-1.0%. The default hook was backed out; coalesced mixed-prefix batches stay on the non-overlap path.

cells.label: llama-launch-order-moreblocks-negative
cells.notes: CPU-first launch gives only a tiny lift at 4/6 suffix blocks and does not rescue earlier splits. More CPU suffix blocks raise effective capacity but reduce throughput sharply on this workload.

cells.label: llama-eager-0.72-total896-split768-negative-repeat
cells.notes: Out-of-sample total896 capacity-window probe. Hybrid split768 raises effective capacity by 16.7% but still loses on repeated means because 8 active CPU suffix blocks are too expensive over the longer decode window.

cells.label: llama-eager-0.80-total896-split864-negative
cells.notes: Out-of-sample total896 capacity-window probe. Best mem0.80 hybrid frontier point was split864, but the 3.7% capacity gain with 2 active CPU suffix blocks still lost to GPU-only.

cells.label: qwen-graph-0.67-total896-split736-negative
cells.notes: Out-of-sample total896 capacity-window probe. Best Qwen graph hybrid point was split736, but the 13.3% capacity gain with 10 active CPU suffix blocks still lost to GPU-only.

diagnostics.label: llama-eager-0.80-total1024-split1008-coalesced-prefix-ab
diagnostics.notes: Matched rerun. Coalesced prefix helps relative to split row path but still loses to GPU-only; capacity gain is only 22.97/22.61 = 1.6%.

diagnostics.label: llama-flash-attn-lse-b512-seq1024-split1008
diagnostics.notes: LSE materialization is effectively free at this shape and does not explain the e2e hybrid loss.

diagnostics.label: llama-hybrid-wrapper-noop-b512-seq1024
diagnostics.notes: No-op suffix shows active-suffix row fraction controls whether prefix shortening beats merge/control overhead.

diagnostics.label: llama-eager-0.80-total1024-split960-stats-enabled-diagnostic
diagnostics.notes: Diagnostic only; CUDA timing and stats lower throughput. Confirms real split960 scheduling often has non-tiny active suffix row fractions.

diagnostics.label: llama-merge-artifact-placement-h32d128
diagnostics.notes: Explicit H2D artifact copy plus GPU merge is only marginally faster than UVA merge, so artifact-copy revival is not the next throughput lever.

diagnostics.label: llama-wrapper-metadata-skip-after-cleanup
diagnostics.notes: After skipping unused prefix metadata allocation for precomputed-prefix calls, wrapper timings moved only slightly; cleanup is correct but not a throughput unlock.

diagnostics.label: llama-eager-0.80-total1024-split960-scheduler-budget-ab
diagnostics.notes: Coarse max_num_batched_tokens tuning is too blunt: 2048 helps slightly but remains negative; 1024 hurts hybrid more.

diagnostics.label: llama-suffix-runner-hotlen-nosnapshot-curve
diagnostics.notes: Default standalone snapshots overstate the production prepared-runner cost. With production-like no-snapshot staging, the wrapper is close to direct at 32 suffix tokens and has a smaller residual envelope at 64; the main cost still scales with real suffix attention/scatter.

diagnostics.label: qwen-prefill-spillover-eager-profile-20260530
diagnostics.notes: Diagnostic for split-crossing prefill behavior. Prompt rows at positions >= split_tokens naturally take the CPU suffix path; this is not an all-GPU prefill followed by a separate spill copy. The B8 spillover case is throughput-negative because it is below the GPU-only capacity frontier and exposes row-expanded spillover overhead.

diagnostics.label: llama-eager-0.72-split640-scheduler-shape

cases.instrumentation_note: CUDA timing and COTS diagnostics perturb throughput; use headline_non_stats for policy ratio.

diagnostics.notes: Stats-enabled fill-in for the lower-memory Llama promoted cell. Hybrid increases effective capacity from 9.98x to 11.97x and changes the first logged wave from 40 running GPU-only requests to 147 running hybrid requests. Most active hybrid windows are all-suffix rows; mixed prefix/suffix rows appear only when newer requests are admitted while older requests are already past the split.
